engineGuide.h2: Peugeot Expert Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot Expert across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced 2007 in MK3 Expert; common-rail 16v diesel with variable geometry turbo, but rear-mounted timing chain caused widespread replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi 90, 1.6 HDi 115, Panel Van 1.6 HDi 90, Panel Van 1.6 HDi 115, Tepee 1.6 HDi
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (60k-100k miles)
Turbo oil feed gauze blockage causing starvation
Injector seal leaks leading to hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6TED4 Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Updated DV6 variant with improved EGR and DPF systems; retained timing chain design but with revised tensioner for better longevity.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi 115, Panel Van 1.6 HDi 115, Professional 1.6 HDi
commonFailures: DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles
EGR valve carbon buildup reducing power
Turbo actuator sticking causing limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6C Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 6 compliant DV6 derivative with updated emissions hardware; used in late MK3 and early MK4 Experts before BlueHDi transition.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi 115 Euro 6, Panel Van 1.6 HDi 115 Euro 6
commonFailures: AdBlue system injector failure
NOx sensor electrical faults
DPF differential pressure sensor errors
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6FC Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: BlueHDi evolution of the DV6 with SCR/AdBlue technology; improved fuel economy but added complexity for emissions compliance.
commonFailures: AdBlue pump failure causing no-start condition
SCR catalyst efficiency below threshold
Timing chain guide wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for BH01 Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Robust 8v diesel introduced in MK2 Expert; known for durability but susceptible to injector and head gasket issues on high-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 HDi 98, 2.0 HDi 110, Panel Van 2.0 HDi, Tepee 2.0 HDi
commonFailures: Injector seal failure causing diesel knock
Head gasket failure above 120k miles
Oil cooler gasket leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10 Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: 16v turbocharged evolution of DW10; the most common 2.0 HDi in MK3 Experts, balancing power and reliability for commercial use.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 HDi 128, Panel Van 2.0 HDi 128, Professional 2.0 HDi 128, Tepee 2.0 HDi 128
commonFailures: DPF clogging on urban/short-trip use
EGR cooler leakage causing coolant loss
Turbo wastegate actuator sticking
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10BTED4 Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: High-output 163hp variant with revised turbo and injection; popular for heavier payloads but requires stricter maintenance to avoid issues.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 HDi 163, Panel Van 2.0 HDi 163, Professional 2.0 HDi 163, Automatic 2.0 HDi 163
commonFailures: Twin-mass flywheel wear causing vibration
High-pressure fuel pump wear above 100k miles
EGR valve carbon buildup reducing response
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10CB Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 6 transitional 2.0 HDi with updated emissions hardware; bridged the gap between DW10BTED4 and full BlueHDi systems.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration failure on stop-start use
NOx storage catalyst efficiency faults
Turbo variable vane sticking
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10CTED4 Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Full BlueHDi system with SCR/AdBlue; the current-generation 2.0 diesel offering best-in-class emissions compliance for MK4 Experts.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 BlueHDi 150, 2.0 BlueHDi 180, Professional 2.0 BlueHDi, Active 2.0 BlueHDi
commonFailures: AdBlue tank heater element failure
SCR injector clogging causing no-start
DPF ash accumulation requiring cleaning
entries.cta: Get quotes for AH01 Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Naturally aspirated indirect injection diesel; simple, robust design favoured by high-mileage commercial users despite lower power output.
compatibleVariants: 1.9 D, 1.9 D Panel Van, 1.9 D Tepee
commonFailures: Oil pickup gauze blockage causing starvation
Injection pump wear leading to hard starting
Head gasket failure on overheated units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW8 Peugeot Expert engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Pre-DW8 indirect injection diesel used in early MK1 Experts; renowned for mechanical simplicity and ease of repair.
compatibleVariants: 1.9 D MK1, 1.9 D Panel Van MK1
commonFailures: Injection pump timing drift causing poor running
Cylinder head cracking on overheated engines
Oil seal leaks at crank and cam positions
entries.cta: Get quotes for XUD9 Peugeot Expert engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot Expert Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot Expert's diesel engines are workhorses but not immune to failure - the 1.6 HDi DV6 family is most affected by timing chain and turbo issues, while the 2.0 HDi DW10 range faces DPF and injector challenges. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi Timing Chain Failure - Why DV6 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi 90, 1.6 HDi 115 (2007-2016, DV6TED4 & DV6C)
problems.rootCause: Rear-mounted timing chain tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes rattling, skipped teeth, and catastrophic valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; no valve damage yet

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head repair, valve replacement, new chain kit
repairOptions.longevity: Only if block is undamaged; limited lifespan extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DV6 engine with upgraded tensioner, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for commercial users; addresses root cause permanently

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold start from your 1.6 HDi Expert, act immediately - a preventative chain kit replacement is far cheaper than a full rebuild. Once valves are bent, replacement is usually the only economical option for a commercial van.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6 engine prices for your Peugeot Expert

problems.h4: Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi Turbo Failure - Oil Starvation from Clogged Gauze
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi 90, 1.6 HDi 115, Tepee 1.6 HDi (2007-2016, DV6TED4 & DV6C)
problems.rootCause: Tiny oil feed gauze filter at turbo inlet clogs with carbon, starving the turbo of lubrication and causing bearing seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, clean housing, replace banjo gauze filter
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if turbo bearings are still intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned turbo with updated gauze design
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if engine block is healthy; 2-3 year expectancy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DV6 engine with new turbo, upgraded oil feed, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vans; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: If your Expert has lost power with blue smoke and whining noise, the turbo is likely seized. For vans over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded oil feed is often better value than turbo-only replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DV6 turbo replacement Peugeot Expert engine

problems.h4: Peugeot Expert DPF Problems - Clogging on Short-Journey 2.0 HDi Use
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Expert 2.0 HDi 128, 2.0 HDi 163 (2007-2016, DW10BTED4 & DW10CB)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short trips prevent DPF regeneration; soot accumulation increases backpressure, reducing power and eventually causing engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, high-speed run or chemical clean
repairOptions.longevity: Only works if DPF isn't physically blocked; temporary fix

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or cleaned DPF unit with sensors recalibrated
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine is otherwise healthy; 3-5 year expectancy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with DPF
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DW10 engine with new DPF, updated ECU mapping, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial vans; addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: If your Expert is used for local deliveries, schedule regular motorway runs to aid DPF regeneration. Once the DPF is physically blocked, replacement is unavoidable - consider a rebuilt engine if mileage exceeds 120k.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DW10 engine prices for your Peugeot Expert

problems.h4: Peugeot Expert Injector Failure - Diesel Knock and Hard Starting
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Expert 2.0 HDi 98, 2.0 HDi 128 (2000-2016, DW10 & DW10BTED4)
problems.rootCause: Injector seals degrade allowing diesel leakage into cylinders; causes hard starting, rough running, and potential hydro-lock damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace copper seals and washers, recode
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if injectors themselves are still functional

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned injectors with coding and calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if engine block is healthy; 2-4 year expectancy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Injectors
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DW10 engine with new injectors, updated seals, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vans; eliminates repeat injector issues

problems.recommendation: If your Expert has diesel knock or white smoke on startup, test injector leak-off first. For vans approaching 120k miles, a rebuilt engine with new injectors often offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DW10 injector replacement Peugeot Expert engine

problems.group: Peugeot Expert Oil Pickup Gauze Blockage
problems.h4: Peugeot Expert Oil Pickup Gauze Blockage - Turbo Starvation Prevention
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi 90, 1.6 HDi 115 (2007-2016, DV6TED4 & DV6C)
problems.rootCause: Small mesh gauze in turbo oil feed line clogs with carbon deposits, restricting flow and causing turbo bearing seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove banjo bolt, clean gauze, flush oil system, replace filter
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; must be done before turbo damage occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new turbo with upgraded gauze design, full oil system clean
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; updated gauze reduces recurrence risk

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Feed
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DV6 engine with upgraded oil feed design, new turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vans; permanently addresses oil starvation risk

problems.recommendation: This is a preventable failure - if you own a 1.6 HDi Expert, have the gauze inspected at every service. Once the turbo is damaged, replacement is the only option; consider a rebuilt engine if mileage exceeds 90k.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6 engine prices with upgraded oil feed for your Peugeot Expert

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot Expert
finalCta.paragraph: From timing chain rattles on the 1.6 HDi to DPF clogging on the 2.0 HDi, Peugeot Expert engines face real challenges - but replacement doesn't mean dealer prices. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines with transparent pricing, 12-month warranties, and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get your Expert back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot Expert Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot Expert and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Peugeotmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Peugeot Expert Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot Expert's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: MK1 Launch: XUD9 & DW8 Diesels Debut
years.description: The original Expert launched with simple, robust indirect-injection diesels favoured for mechanical simplicity and ease of repair.
keyChanges: Panel van and Tepee passenger variants available
knownFor: Mechanical simplicity and ease of DIY repair
Injection pump timing drift on high-mileage units
Cylinder head cracking if overheated
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995-2000 Peugeot Expert replacement engines

years.description: The MK2 facelift introduced the more powerful and efficient DW10 2.0 HDi, becoming the preferred engine for commercial users.
keyChanges: DW10 2.0 HDi replaced older 1.9D as main engine option
Improved emissions compliance for Euro 3/4 standards
knownFor: DW10 robustness and commercial suitability
Injector seal failure causing diesel knock above 100k miles
Head gasket failure on overheated high-mileage units

keyChanges: New platform shared with Citroen Dispatch and Fiat Scudo
DV6 1.6 HDi introduced (90hp, 115hp) with rear-mounted timing chain
DW10 updated to BTED4 and CB variants with 128hp and 163hp outputs
DPF and EGR systems added for Euro 4/5 compliance
knownFor: DV6 timing chain tensioner failures 60k-100k miles
DV6 turbo oil feed gauze blockage causing starvation
DW10 DPF clogging on urban commercial use
High replacement demand due to commercial downtime pressure

faq.h2: Peugeot Expert Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot Expert engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine costs range from £950 for a 1.6 HDi DV6 to £3,800 for a 2.0 BlueHDi AH01, supply only. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 depending on garage location and engine complexity.
keyPoints: 1.6 HDi DV6 rebuilt: £950-£2,600 supply only
2.0 HDi DW10 rebuilt: £1,100-£2,900 supply only
2.0 BlueHDi AH01 rebuilt: £1,400-£3,800 supply only
Supply & fit typically adds £400-£900 labour
All prices include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Expert engine replacement prices UK

items.question: Is the Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi engine reliable?
items.answer: The 1.6 HDi DV6 family has known weaknesses - timing chain tensioner failure and turbo oil feed gauze blockage are common between 60k-100k miles, driving high replacement demand.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner fails 60k-100k miles on DV6TED4
Turbo oil feed gauze clogs causing bearing seizure
Preventative chain kit replacement costs £550-£950
Rebuilt DV6 engines include upgraded tensioner design
Regular oil changes critical to extend DV6 lifespan
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi engines

items.question: What causes Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi timing chain failure?
items.answer: The DV6's rear-mounted timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes rattling, skipped teeth, and bent valves if not addressed.
keyPoints: Hydraulic tensioner fails due to oil pressure loss
Chain slack causes rattling noise on cold start
Skipped teeth bend valves requiring head repair or replacement
Upgraded tensioner designs available in rebuilt units
Preventative replacement recommended at 80k miles
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Expert DV6 timing chain replacement options

items.question: Can I fit a Citroen Dispatch engine in a Peugeot Expert?
items.answer: Yes - the Expert shares its platform with Citroen Dispatch and Fiat Scudo, so DV6, DW10, BH01 and AH01 engines are interchangeable with correct ECU coding.
keyPoints: Expert, Dispatch and Scudo share identical engine bays
DV6, DW10, BH01 and AH01 codes fit all three models
ECU coding required for immobiliser compatibility
Verify engine code matches before purchasing replacement
Specialist garages can handle cross-model coding
items.cta: Find compatible Peugeot Expert replacement engines from Dispatch/Scudo donors

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Peugeot Expert engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a Peugeotmum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK garage
Used engines typically carry shorter 3-6 month warranties
Always confirm warranty terms with your chosen supplier
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Expert engine warranties from UK specialists

items.question: How long does a Peugeot Expert engine replacement take?
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines usually in stock for DV6 and DW10 codes
BlueHDi AH01/BH01 may require 3-5 day lead time
Supply & fit installations typically 1-3 days total
Commercial users often prioritise rapid turnaround over lowest price
Nationwide delivery available to your chosen garage
items.cta: Find Peugeot Expert engine supply & fit deals with rapid turnaround

items.question: What's the difference between Peugeot Expert 1.6 HDi and 2.0 HDi?
items.answer: The 1.6 HDi DV6 is lighter and more fuel-efficient but has known timing chain and turbo issues; the 2.0 HDi DW10 is heavier but more robust and preferred for high-mileage commercial use.
keyPoints: 1.6 HDi: 90-120hp, better fuel economy, higher failure rate
2.0 HDi: 98-163hp, more torque, better long-term reliability
DV6 timing chain failures common 60k-100k miles
DW10 DPF issues mainly on urban/short-trip use
Many owners upgrade from 1.6 to 2.0 on replacement
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Expert 1.6 vs 2.0 HDi replacement engine prices

items.question: Where do I find my Peugeot Expert engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the engine block near the oil filter housing, listed on the V5C logbook, or visible on the VIN plate under the bonnet.
keyPoints: Check V5C logbook section V.5 for engine code
Stamped on engine block near oil filter housing
VIN plate under bonnet may show engine derivative
DV6, DW10, BH01 or AH01 are common Expert codes
Enter registration on our site to auto-identify your engine
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot Expert engine code instantly via registration

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot Expert engine or buying a new van?
items.answer: For Experts valued under £8,000, a rebuilt engine at £950-£3,800 usually offers better value than purchasing a newer van, especially with a 12-month warranty included.
keyPoints: Rebuilt DV6 engines from £950 vs £15k+ for newer van
12-month warranty reduces post-replacement risk
Commercial users often prefer known vehicle history
Factor in downtime costs when comparing options
EngineMarket quotes help benchmark replacement vs purchase
items.cta: Get tailored Peugeot Expert engine replacement quotes to compare vs van purchase
